</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />

<trait label="Twin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=197" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 279

**Favored Weapon** [Achaekek](/Deities.aspx?ID=325)

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 5 gp

**Damage** 1d6 S

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15)
</row>

</column>

---

The signature weapon of the Red Mantis assassins, this curved blade is serrated like a saw, hence the name.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-15" />

<title level="2">Specific Magic Weapons</title>
[Blood-Drinker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3485), [Bloodstride Blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4961), [Crimson Bluff](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3487), [Fleshrender](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3488)
name: Sawtooth Saber

You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

    level="1"
    right="Weapon 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Deadly d10" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=174" />
<trait label="Propulsive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=191" />
<trait label="Volley 30 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=201" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 128, [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 280

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 20 gp

**Damage** 1d8 P

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1+

**Range** 100 ft.

**Reload** 0
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Arrows](/Weapons.aspx?ID=443)

</column>

---

This projectile weapon is made from horn, wood, and sinew laminated together to increase the power of its pull and the force of its projectile. Like all longbows, its great size also increases the bow's range and power. You must use two hands to fire it, and it cannot be used while mounted. Any time an ability is specifically restricted to a longbow, such as [Erastil's](/Deities.aspx?ID=6) favored weapon, it also applies to composite longbows unless otherwise stated.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />
name: Composite Longbow

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Backpack Ballista](/Weapons.aspx?ID=447)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 62

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 18 gp

**Damage** 1d12 P

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1+

**Range** 180 ft.

**Reload** 1 minute
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Crossbow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

</column>

---

This complex wooden device, worn on the back, contains a miniature ballista on a retractable arm. As an Interact action, you can pull a lever to deploy or retract the ballista. As long as it remains deployed, you must hold the ballista using that hand or some of the components spill out onto the ground, just like dropping any other weapon. While deployed, the device opens and raises the ballista up over your shoulder. While retracted, the ballista and its mount slide down and are concealed within the device. Although a backpack ballista packs a punch, the device is a challenge to operate. Reloading it takes 1 minute and can't be done while worn. As normal, you can't wear a backpack ballista with another backpack.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-17" />

A common cutting tool, an adze resembles an axe—but the cutting edge is horizontal, rather than vertical. The adze’s shape makes it popular among woodworkers, and tripkee builders often use them to construct their treetop homes. The tool also serves as an effective weapon, due in part to the immense strength required to swing it.

You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

This kobold wire is thin and hard to see, making it perfect for an ambush. The wielder flicks one handle around a vulnerable spot so that it serves as a catch for the wire, then yanks the other handle back, pulling the wire tight and inflicting painful lacerations. The function and name come from a wire-based trap called a “slow fang.”

A fighting oar is a sturdy boat oar, typically made of wood, whose haft and blades are reinforced for use in combat. A fighting oar adds its item bonus from weapon potency runes (if any) as an item bonus on Piloting [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) and Sailing Lore checks made to pilot a rowed vehicle (for more information on vehicles, see [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3117)).

You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Combat Fishing Pole](/Weapons.aspx?ID=508)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Ranged Trip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=681" />
<trait label="Tethered" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=804" />
<trait label="thrown 20 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=711" />
<trait label="Versatile P" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=724" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 147

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 4 sp

**Damage** 1d8 B

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Fishing Lure](/Weapons.aspx?ID=515)

</column>

---

The combat fishing pole is a sturdy and flexible pole that can be used as a weapon in melee. When a lure is attached to a combat fishing pole, it can be used to make thrown weapon attacks with a range increment of 20 feet, though the lure must then be reeled back in with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action before the weapon can be used at range again (see the tethered trait). The ranged trip, tethered, thrown, and versatile P traits of the combat fishing pole only apply to thrown attacks made with a lure; throwing the pole itself leaves you with no practical way to retrieve it other than moving to its location and picking it up. A combat fishing pole can be used alongside fishing tackle to fish, and it adds its item bonus from weapon potency runes (if any) as an item bonus on checks to fish when used in this manner.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-7" />

---

When a lure is attached to a combat fishing pole, it can be used to make thrown weapon attacks with a range increment of 20 feet, though the lure must then be reeled back in with an Interact action before the weapon can be used at range again (see the tethered trait). The ranged trip, tethered, thrown, and versatile P traits of the combat fishing pole only apply to thrown attacks made with a lure; throwing the pole itself leaves you with no practical way to retrieve it other than moving to its location and picking it up.

Though less powerful than a blunderbuss, the dragonmouth pistol is appreciated for its portability and onehanded design. The name was coined because of the destructive belch of this handheld scatter weapon, but many gunsmiths craft dragon-mouth pistols with elaborate embellishments that resemble a stylized dragon's maw framing the barrel.

**Bulk** 1

---

Hand cannons are little more than a hardened tube with a handle and external ignition attached. A hand cannon can be used to fire almost anything that can be packed into its barrel. The wielder of a hand cannon can change the damage type granted by its modular trait as part of the same Interact action used to reload.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

**Bulk** 1

As [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=59) poured out of [the Darklands](/Rules.aspx?ID=2993) following their Quest for Sky, the [orcs](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=66) driven before them adapted to their tactics—including dwarves’ heavily armored infantry charges. Surviving orcs developed a response: a weapon that combines a [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365) and [maul](/Weapons.aspx?ID=388) to crush heavy armor and skewer charging foes. Although rarely seen today, the weapon is remarkably effective in the right situation.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-8" />

This reinforced lute is suitable both for use as a handheld [musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737) and for bashing heads should a crowd turn sour. Its strings are finely braided wires that run along its sturdy metal neck. A battle lute adds its item bonus from weapon potency  runes (if any) as an item bonus on [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks made while using it as an instrument.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

A scourge—also known as a cat-o'-nine tails—is a set of several knotted cords made from cotton or leather and attached to a handle. While most scourges are more suitable for torture than combat, when fashioned into a weapon, a scourge can have metal barbs woven into the cords to pierce clothing and armor.

A dagger attached to a retractable mechanism is integrated in this gauntlet's dorsum, so a combatant can quickly arm themself with a blade to exploit the weak points in an enemy's armor. Switching configurations on the gauntlet reveals or retracts the contained dagger as appropriate. The dagger isn't removable, and thus can't be wielded or etched with runes separately from the gauntlet.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Backpack Catapult](/Weapons.aspx?ID=544)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Volley 50 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=730" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 63

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 20 gp

**Damage** 1d12 B

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1+

**Range** 240 ft.

**Reload** 1 minute
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Crossbow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

</column>

---

This wooden device is worn on the back and contains a miniature catapult mounted on a retractable frame. As an Interact action, you can pull a lever to deploy or retract the catapult. As long as it remains deployed, you must hold the catapult using that hand or some of the components spill out onto the ground, just like dropping any other weapon. While deployed, the device opens and raises the catapult up over your shoulder. While retracted, the catapult and its mount slide down and are concealed within the device. A backpack catapult fires specialized stone spheres that are loaded into the bucket while unworn and retracted, through a sliding hatch; the reloading process takes 1 minute. To prevent misfires and accidental injury, the bucket fully encloses the stone while deployed, only opening when fired. As normal, you can’t wear a backpack catapult with another backpack.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-17" />
name: Backpack Catapult

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pantograph Gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=547)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Deadly d6" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=570" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Reach" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=684" />
<trait label="Shove" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=694" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 63

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 2 gp

**Damage** 1d4 B

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

</column>

---

A pantograph gauntlet is a heavy, fist-like weight, mounted on an extendable frame and attached to your outer arm with a series of leather straps. The frame’s set of mechanical linkages connected at various hinges allow movements to propagate across the frame based on reshaping parallelograms, further controlled by a crossbar grasped in your hand. A pantograph gauntlet is driven by your own motion and mirrors your arm’s movements—a punch thrown with your fist moves the pantograph, extending the weight out at a rapid speed to land blows up to 10 feet away. In some regions, such as Alkenstar and Ustalav, pantograph gauntlets are occasionally constructed entirely of metal and fashioned in the likeness of oversized arms, incorporating a complex system of gears or a miniature steam engine in place of the simpler pantograph mechanism.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-4" />

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Deadly D6" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=174" />

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Simple

**Group** [Spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14)
</row>

---

Developed by Alkenstar scouts who often don’t have time to break down a tripod when beset by the chaotic mutant monsters of the Mana Wastes, this two-pronged stabbing weapon can be used as a bipod to stabilize a gun with potent kickback. A forked bipod can be deployed or retrieved for use as a melee weapon as an Interact action.

This blade or spike can be attached to a [crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=425) or [firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16) but, unlike other attached weapons, can be wielded in one hand as its own weapon.     level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Air Repeater](/Weapons.aspx?ID=565)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Repeating" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=374" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 151

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 5 gp

**Damage** 1d4 P

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

**Range** 30 ft.

**Reload** 0
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Simple

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Magazine (Air Repeater)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=566)

</column>

---

A thin-barreled firearm that uses a container of pressurized air instead of black powder to propel small metal bullets from an attached cartridge, the air repeater has fallen out of common use in Arcadia due to its poor stopping power, though it’s still used occasionally for casual hunting and sport shooting. The air repeater and its longer-ranged, two-handed variant are still valued by some for their ability to allow a shooter to fire multiple rounds without needing to stop to reload or crank to a new chamber. A typical air repeater magazine holds 6 pellets.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />

This amazingly simple projectile weapon is nothing more than a metal tube packed with black powder and a stopper, attached to the sharpened head of a javelin. A loaded fire lance can be wielded as a normal spear, though it requires an Interact action to regrip the weapon and hold it properly when switching from one use to another. Fire lances are most commonly found in Tian Xia, though occasionally one makes its way all the way to Avistan, typically in the hands of a Tien mercenary or caravan guard.

</title>

---

The most commonly available of firearms in Alkenstar, the flintlock musket includes an external firing mechanism and an efficient and relatively compact frame. Though lacking the range and firing power of the arquebus preferred by Alkenstar and Dongun Hold’s military members, the flintlock musket is popular among civilians for its ease of use.

This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

Though less accurate and powerful than a flintlock musket, the flintlock pistol is a preferred weapon of privateers thanks to its more compact size and affordability. Pirate captains often wear a brace of such pistols in a bandolier so they can draw and fire without stopping to reload.

Popular among privateers and mercenaries in Goka, hand cannons are little more than a hardened tube with a handle and external ignition attached. A hand cannon can be used to fire almost anything that can be packed into its barrel. Like the one-handed air repeater, this thin-barreled firearm uses a container of pressurized air instead of black powder to propel small metal pellets from an attached cartridge. The long air repeater has better range and ammo capacity than the one-handed variant, though it still lacks significant stopping power. A typical long air repeater magazine holds 8 pellets.

Losing or being forced to surrender their clan pistol is a terrible embarrassment for the dwarves that carry them.

</title>

---

Similar to the blunderbuss, a dragon-mouth pistol fires pellets from a flared barrel. Though less powerful than a [blunderbuss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=196), the dragon-mouth pistol is appreciated for its portability and one-handed design. Though the name was coined because of the destructive belch of this handheld scatter weapon, many gunsmiths craft dragon-mouth pistols with elaborate embellishments that resemble a stylized dragon’s maw framing the barrel.

Made for settling disputes when diplomacy fails, dueling pistols are finely crafted and made to fit easily into a holster or pocket. A favored weapon of monster hunters in Arcadia, the harmona gun is a large-bore long gun that fires a heavy, slow-moving round. Jezails are simple, efficient long guns developed in Casmaron that typically feature a custom stock and a flintlock firing mechanism. Though lacking the range and stopping power of an arquebus or the raw force of a [harmona](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1190) gun, the jezail is an elegant, well-balanced weapon suitable for a variety of combat situations. It’s even possible to tuck it under one arm to fire a less accurate attack that uses only one hand.

[Dwarven Scattergun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=586)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concussive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=401" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Kickback" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=409" />
<trait label="scatter 10 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=413" />

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">

**Damage** 1d8 P

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Range** 50 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (5 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=589)

</column>

---

A favored weapon of [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=59) scouts from Dongun Hold, the dwarven scattergun is a powerful weapon designed to take advantage of a dwarf’s sturdy frame. A dwarven scattergun fires a large paper cartridge stuffed with black powder and knuckle-sized lumps of metal, creating a devastating burst so destructive that a foolish dwarf might find themself catching painful ricochets when firing at a too-close target. Some scatterguns are crafted with a clockwork firing tray that can quickly sort and load black powder and shot without needing a prepackaged cartridge, though this is largely an aesthetic feature with no real mechanical benefit.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

</title>

---

A flingflenser is a [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62)-designed weapon ending in an ovoid tube with a hatch and handle on the narrow end. A cluster of circular blades held together and attached to a black powder packet with a thin leather strap serves as ammunition and is loaded through the hatch before being fired with a flintlock or other external ignition mechanism. The flingflenser’s sturdy design also places it among the more reliable goblin weapons.

>
[Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=588)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 151

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 1 sp

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Ammunition

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

</column>

---

Firearms require ammunition consisting of a projectile and black powder. A round of ammo can vary in its composition but is typically either a prepackaged paper cartridge, including wadding, bullet, and black powder, or loose shot packed in manually. Some weapons, like hand cannons and blunderbusses, can fire other materials, but their ammunition has the same Price due to the cost of the black powder. Because making rounds of firearm ammunition requires creating black powder, you need the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5117) skill feat to make them. Firearm rounds are a valid option for magical ammunition, just like arrows or bolts. [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) magical firearm ammunition requires you to be able to craft both alchemical and magical items.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />

<traits>

</row>

---

This weapon, favored by [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=59) and those who like smashing and shooting, takes the form of a stoutly built gun designed similarly to an [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=518) with a hammer head built into the muzzle, decreasing [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847) but limiting range.

</traits>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=588)

</column>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Hands** 1

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

At first glance this weapon looks like nothing more than an iron-bound club. But the top of the weapon features a latch that opens to reveal three rotating pistol muzzles concealed in the mace’s head that can be fired and rotated using triggers built into the weapon’s haft.

<traits>

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 12 gp

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Hands** 2

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

A recently developed weapon created for an elven champion from Jinin, the three-peaked tree can be used as both a trident and a mithral tree. A three-peaked tree has a length of silken rope attached to the butt of its haft, allowing it to be quickly retrieved after thrown.

</column>

<trait label="Elf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=58" />

<trait label="Parry" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=190" />

**Reload** 1
</row>

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=588)

</column>

An explosive dogslicer is a sneaky, explosive weapon that often brings perverse joy to the [goblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62) who use them. At first glance, it appears to be a triple-bladed [dogslicer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=405) with an oversized guard.

[Spoon Gun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=603)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cobbled" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=400" />
<trait label="Goblin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=81" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 157

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">

**Damage** 1d6 Modular

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=588)

</column>

---

No one’s entirely certain who developed the spoon gun, but all authorities agree that it was probably a [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62). Essentially a terrible idea in firearm form, the spoon gun is a spring‑powered hand cannon with a modified grip that uses miscellaneous knives, forks, chopsticks, and spoons as ammunition. Users typically upend the entire contents of their cutlery drawer into the gun, aim it in the general direction of the foe, and hope it hits something. 

This [hand cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=523) is a martial weapon, instead of a simple weapon. It has the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) (5 feet) trait and uses cutlery or similar-sized objects as ammunition instead of bullets (enough cheap cutlery to fire ten shots costs 1 sp).

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />
name: Spoon Gun

Developed by a [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62) weaponsmith who missed the ‘hand’ part of ‘hand cannon’, this comically-oversized gun has a heavily reinforced barrel and is loaded with a worrisome quantity of gunpowder.

This hand cannon is a martial weapon, instead of a simple weapon. It has the [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=597) trait and a range increment of 20 feet. It also has the following modified critical failure condition: 

**Critical Failure** The attack misses, the weapon misfires, and you take 1d12 fire damage as it explodes in your face.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

The battle saddle is a special saddle for a mount that has two large, winglike blades. These blades normally lie flat alongside the saddle, providing additional protection for the rider, but they can be deployed with a tug on the reins to slash at enemies adjacent to the mount. When using a battle saddle to parry, you can decide whether the circumstance bonus to AC applies to you or to your mount.

Traditionally a favored weapon in Molthune for settling disputes between military leaders, the dancer’s spear has seen a recent resurgence in popularity in the neighboring kingdom of Nirmathas, largely due to its effectiveness at striking down attacking skeletons and other undead from a relatively safe distance. A dancer’s spear has a 7-foot-long wooden haft capped with a triangular metal blade at one end, counterbalanced on the other end with a reinforced metal sleeve that, in a pinch, can be used as an effective striking surface.

[Polytool](/Weapons.aspx?ID=629)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Modular (B, P, or S)" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=271" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 27

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Numeria have access to the polytool._

**Favored Weapon** [Casandalee](/Deities.aspx?ID=335), [Wheels of Innovation](/Deities.aspx?ID=385)

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 10 gp

**Damage** 1d6 Modular

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15)
</row>

</column>

---

The polytool is a small metal rod with a number of simple tools folded inside. The user can extend a long ceramic blade, as well as an awl, chisel, file, flint and steel, hook, inkpen, magnifying glass, pliers, scissors, and a small saw. The flint and steel can be used up to 10 times before needing to be replaced. Though inspired by advanced Numerian technology, the polytool is a simple enough feat of metalworking that any blacksmith could produce it—perfect for the goddess [Casandalee](/Deities.aspx?ID=335) to spread innovation farther than actual Numerian tech could reach.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-15" />
